We had prayer meeting last night. We just want to see God do something. Hallelujah. But can I tell you on Friday, there was a preacher that stood in the pulpit and preached at the chapel on Friday, and I'm not trying to take beef with the guy, but he got up in the pulpit and he said, you guys think you're going to go pastor church, you're going to get 200 people, it's not going to happen? Come on. I understand where he's coming from, his experience, he's been a pastor for 50 years, whatever, but my God is able. He is able to save to the uttermost, Hebrews 7 and verse number 25. He's not willing that any should perish, but that all should come to repentance. And we don't change our position to try to grow a church, but we seek to obey the Lord and follow the Lord, and listen, I still believe that the Bible says that the gospel is the power of God unto salvation. The gospel still works. Listen, if God wasn't working, if God didn't have a plan, we'd just go ahead and close up and just go do something else, honestly. But if we truly believe that God is still on his throne, and he is, and he still wants to do something in 2026, you say, pastor, have you watched the news? I don't watch the news a lot. You know why? It's kind of discouraging. Just honestly. I told you during COVID, if you're going to watch the news for an hour, you better read the Bible for four hours. And I just on the fly made up a one to four ratio. Somebody came to me and said, how'd you figure that out? It just came out. I don't know. But you know, sometimes we spend so much time on the news and on those things that are negative. Listen, our God still reigns. He still has all power, as much as he's ever had. Amen? And he wants to use us. We got to deal with that. We identify it. Last week's message. But when we acknowledge the consequences, it's not benign. It's harmful. It's dangerous. It chokes out. We got to deal with it. And just like the disciples in Mark 9, thank God they came to Jesus and said, why could not we cast him out? And Jesus said, because of your unbelief. And they came to Jesus and they had that private moment and Jesus gave them the truth. We don't know what the God of the universe, our Savior, and say, God, where's the doubt and unbelief in my life? Help me to deal with it. Help me to get rid of it. Help me. Lord, as the disciples prayed in Luke 17, Lord, increase our faith. The church in Thessalonica, their faith groweth exceedingly. So then faith cometh by hearing and hearing by the word of God. We need to deal with doubt because doubt has some serious consequences. Let's pray. Father, we do love you. We thank you so much for loving us. We thank you for your word, the truth contained therein. We thank you for the privilege of being able to study it. Lord, I pray that you'd help me and help us to not only identify doubt, but Lord, help us to acknowledge the serious consequences of doubt so that we will root it out of our lives with your help, by your grace. Lord, I pray that you'd help us to get alone with you and search our hearts. And Lord, that you would search our hearts and show us if there's some area of doubt. Think of that man that said, Lord, I believe. Help thou mine unbelief. Lord, help us to be honest and transparent with you. And God, I pray that you would increase our faith. Thank you for the privilege of prayer. We have our prayer meeting and we think about the blessing of corporate intercessory prayer. And I pray that you'd bless, you'd be honored and glorified. For it's in Christ's name we pray. Amen. And does anyone need a prayer bulletin this evening?
